title: AI-901 Mock Exam 2: Workloads and Responsible AI
description: A 50-question unofficial mock paper aligned to the AI-901 skills measured from April 15, 2026. Includes realistic scenarios, detailed explanations, and official Microsoft Learn references. Original practice content; not official exam questions.

items.prompt: A developer creates an AI app that must search enterprise documents, call an inventory API, and decide whether to create a purchase request. Which workload pattern best describes this solution?
options: Agentic AI
Ground the response with trusted data and validate outputs before use
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required
items.explanation: “Agentic AI” is the best answer because the selection directly satisfies the scenario’s stated requirement. Content Understanding uses analyzers and schemas to turn documents, images, audio, or video into grounded structured output.

items.prompt: A model answers general questions well but hallucinates current company policy. What should the developer add?
items.answer: Grounding with an approved policy data source
options: Grounding with an approved policy data source
Azure AI Search vector indexing without field extraction
A multimodal model prompt without a defined extraction schema
Document Intelligence-style OCR followed by manual review
items.explanation: “Grounding with an approved policy data source” is the best answer because the selection directly satisfies the scenario’s stated requirement. Model capability, deployment choice, and inference settings should be selected against measured quality, latency, cost, and consistency requirements.

references.title: Model catalog and collections in Microsoft Foundry

items.prompt: A legal review assistant must cite only the paragraphs it used from uploaded contracts. Which tool is most relevant?
options: File search with grounded retrieval
Web search
Memory
A custom function or API tool with controlled authentication
items.explanation: “File search with grounded retrieval” is the best answer because the selection directly satisfies the scenario’s stated requirement. Foundry apps and agents combine an appropriate deployment with instructions, SDK or API calls, tools, identity, and evaluation according to the scenario.

items.prompt: A support agent must open tickets through an internal REST API. Which tool type is most appropriate?
items.answer: A custom function or API tool with controlled authentication
options: A custom function or API tool with controlled authentication
Memory
A connected MCP server with scoped tools
File search
items.explanation: “A custom function or API tool with controlled authentication” is the best answer because the selection directly satisfies the scenario’s stated requirement. Foundry apps and agents combine an appropriate deployment with instructions, SDK or API calls, tools, identity, and evaluation according to the scenario.

items.prompt: A developer wants Foundry to run a portal-authored agent with no custom compute to manage. Which option should they choose?
options: Prompt agent
Use a lightweight client that invokes a published agent endpoint
Hosted agent
Call the Responses API directly from the existing process
items.explanation: “Prompt agent” is the best answer because the selection directly satisfies the scenario’s stated requirement. Foundry apps and agents combine an appropriate deployment with instructions, SDK or API calls, tools, identity, and evaluation according to the scenario.

items.prompt: A developer packages custom orchestration as a container and wants Foundry to host it with managed identity and observability. Which option should they choose?
options: Hosted agent
Prompt agent
Call the Responses API directly from the existing process
Create a custom tool hosted outside Foundry and call it from a prompt agent
items.explanation: “Hosted agent” is the best answer because the selection directly satisfies the scenario’s stated requirement. A text or speech solution must use the capability that matches the requested analysis, recognition, translation, or synthesis flow.

items.prompt: A user asks: "Summarize this contract and identify renewal dates." The app passes a PDF directly to a document extraction workflow before summarization. Which service area is involved?
options: Content Understanding for documents
Azure AI Search vector indexing without field extraction
A multimodal model prompt without a defined extraction schema
Document Intelligence-style OCR followed by manual review
items.explanation: “Content Understanding for documents” is the best answer because the selection directly satisfies the scenario’s stated requirement. Content Understanding uses analyzers and schemas to turn documents, images, audio, or video into grounded structured output.

items.prompt: A project needs to process forms, images, audio recordings, and videos with structured outputs. Which Foundry Tools capability is designed for this?
options: Azure Content Understanding
A multimodal model prompt without a defined extraction schema
Document Intelligence-style OCR followed by manual review
Content Understanding with confidence scores and grounding enabled
items.explanation: “Azure Content Understanding” is the best answer because the selection directly satisfies the scenario’s stated requirement. Content Understanding uses analyzers and schemas to turn documents, images, audio, or video into grounded structured output.

items.prompt: A developer must create a lightweight app using Foundry SDKs. Which prerequisite is most relevant according to the exam scope?

options: Familiarity with Azure resources and Python syntax or programming techniques
Use Content Understanding when structured extraction from multimodal content is required

Foundry apps and agents combine an appropriate deployment with instructions, SDK or API calls, tools, identity, and evaluation according to the scenario.

items.prompt: A support team wants to route messages by tone and show the products mentioned in each message. Which TWO text-analysis capabilities are required?
options: Sentiment analysis
Entity recognition
Speech synthesis
Image generation
items.explanation: “Sentiment analysis” and “Entity recognition” are the required choices because the selection directly satisfies the scenario’s stated requirement. The input, required output, and business task identify the workload; unrelated modalities or generative features do not satisfy that requirement.

items.prompt: A developer wants a chat client to call a deployed model in Foundry. Which API pattern is most aligned with current Foundry docs?

Prompt agent

Call the Responses API directly from the existing process

items.prompt: A team is deciding whether to use a multimodal model. Which requirement most strongly indicates that they need one?
items.answer: The prompt includes both text and images
options: The prompt includes both text and images
A text-only chat model with no visual input
Content Understanding image extraction
Image generation from a text prompt
items.explanation: “The prompt includes both text and images” is the best answer because the selection directly satisfies the scenario’s stated requirement. Vision solutions use visual-capable models or services when image content must be interpreted or generated.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: A team wants users to know when they are interacting with AI and what the AI can and cannot do. Which responsible AI principle is most relevant?
options: Transparency
Accountability
Fairness
Reliability and safety
items.explanation: “Transparency” is the best answer because the selection directly satisfies the scenario’s stated requirement. Responsible AI decisions should address the relevant principle in the design and operation of the solution, rather than treating governance as a final UI step.
